question: [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that IdLib.toId() encodes market.loanToken by address only (not by behavior), create a market whose loanToken is a fee-on-transfer token, then trigger take() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(offer.market.loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) pulls less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets into Midnight, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover withdrawable assets and claimableSettlementFee, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where withdrawable + claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] exceeds actual ERC20 balance? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer loanToken, create market via touchMarket, execute take with units=1e18, assert address(midnight).balance(loanToken) < marketState.withdrawable + claimableSettlementFee[loanToken].

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that IdLib.toId() encodes collateralParams[i].token by address only, supply a fee-on-transfer collateral token so that position[id][borrower].collateral[i] is credited with assets but Midnight receives assets*(1-fee), violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual deposited token balance, causing scoped impact: inflated collateral value enabling undercollateralized borrowing and bad debt? Proof idea: deploy a 2% fee-on-transfer collateral token, call supplyCollateral(market, 0, 1e18, borrower), assert position.collateral[0] == 1e18 but ERC20.balanceOf(midnight) increased by only 0.98e18, then take a sell offer and assert isHealthy returns true despite actual undercollateralization.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender, under the precondition that the market loanToken is fee-on-transfer and IdLib.toId() does not encode fee behavior, call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.loanToken, receiver, units) sends units but receiver receives units*(1-fee), violating the invariant that lender credit redemption must deliver face value, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where lender credit is burned but receiver receives less than expected, with the fee silently deducted from the receiver? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate in [1,500] bps, call withdraw for full credit balance, assert receiver.balance(loanToken) < units withdrawn.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that loanToken is fee-on-transfer and repay() calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(market.loanToken, payer, address(this), units) after already decrementing position[id][onBehalf].debt by units, trigger a state where debt is reduced by units but Midnight receives only units*(1-fee), violating the invariant that marketState.withdrawable increase must equal actual token received, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where withdrawable exceeds actual loanToken balance? Proof idea: set fee=1%, call repay(market, 1000e18, borrower, address(0), ''), assert marketState.withdrawable increased by 1000e18 but loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) increased by only 990e18.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the collateral token is fee-on-transfer and IdLib.toId() encodes collateralParams[collateralIndex].token by address only, call liquidate() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.collateralParams[collateralIndex].token, receiver, seizedAssets) sends seizedAssets but receiver receives seizedAssets*(1-fee), violating the invariant that liquidator must receive the full incentive-adjusted collateral, causing scoped impact: liquidation incentive erosion making liquidations unprofitable and leaving unhealthy positions unliquidated? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateral, create undercollateralized position, call liquidate with seizedAssets=maxSeizable, assert receiver.balance(collateral) < seizedAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / flashLoan] Can an unprivileged callback receiver, under the precondition that a token in the flashLoan tokens array is fee-on-transfer, trigger flashLoan() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(tokens[i], callback, assets[i]) sends assets[i] but callback receives assets[i]*(1-fee), then SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(tokens[i], callback, address(this), assets[i]) pulls the full assets[i] from callback, violating the invariant that Midnight's balance must not decrease after a flash loan, causing scoped impact: protocol balance drain by fee amount per flash loan? Proof idea: deploy 1% fee-on-transfer token, call flashLoan([token], [1000e18], callback, ''), assert midnight.balance(token) decreased by 10e18 after repayment.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer, under the precondition that loanToken is fee-on-transfer and claimSettlementFee() calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) after decrementing claimableSettlementFee[token] by amount, trigger a state where claimableSettlementFee is reduced by amount but receiver receives amount*(1-fee), violating the invariant that fee accounting must match actual token transfers, causing scoped impact: fee accounting desync where claimableSettlementFee underestimates actual claimable amount relative to balance? Proof idea: accumulate settlement fees via multiple takes, call claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver), assert receiver.balance < amount and claimableSettlementFee[token] was decremented by full amount.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer, under the precondition that loanToken is fee-on-transfer and claimContinuousFee() decrements continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable by amount before calling SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(market.loanToken, receiver, amount), trigger a state where all three accounting fields are reduced by amount but receiver receives amount*(1-fee), violating the invariant that withdrawable must equal actual redeemable loanToken balance, causing scoped impact: insolvency where remaining lenders cannot fully withdraw? Proof idea: fuzz amount in [1, continuousFeeCredit], call claimContinuousFee, assert sum of all lender credits > loanToken.balanceOf(midnight).

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: storeInCode / touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that IdLib.storeInCode() uses CREATE2 with chainId as salt and the same Market struct, cause a DoS on market creation by pre-deploying a contract at the CREATE2 address computed by keccak256(abi.encodePacked(SSTORE2_PREFIX, abi.encode(market))) with chainId=INITIAL_CHAIN_ID, so that storeInCode() returns address(0) and reverts with SStore2DeploymentFailed(), violating the invariant that any valid market must be touchable, causing scoped impact: permanent fund freeze for any market whose CREATE2 address is griefed? Proof idea: compute CREATE2 address for target market params, deploy a contract there, call touchMarket(market) and assert it reverts with SStore2DeploymentFailed.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (callback path)] Can an unprivileged maker, under the precondition that offer.callback is set to a contract that reverts in IBuyCallback.onBuy(), cause take() to revert after position state has been partially updated (buyerPos.credit increased, sellerPos.debt increased, consumed[maker][group] incremented, claimableSettlementFee incremented) but before SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom is called, violating the invariant that partial state updates must not persist on revert, causing scoped impact: no persistent state corruption due to EVM revert semantics, but confirming the callback revert path fully unwinds all state? Proof idea: deploy a reverting IBuyCallback, call take(), assert all position fields and consumed are unchanged after revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / repay (callback path)] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that repay() decrements position[id][onBehalf].debt and increments marketState.withdrawable before calling IRepayCallback.onRepay(), use a callback that reverts to cause repay() to revert after debt has been decremented but before the loanToken transferFrom, violating the invariant that debt reduction must be atomic with token receipt, causing scoped impact: no persistent desync due to revert, but confirming that a malicious callback can DoS repayment for a specific borrower if the callback address is attacker-controlled? Proof idea: set callback to a reverting contract, call repay(market, units, borrower, revertingCallback, ''), assert position.debt unchanged and marketState.withdrawable unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / liquidate (callback path)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that liquidate() sends seized collateral to receiver before calling ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate() and then pulls repaidUnits from payer, use a callback that reverts to cause liquidate() to revert after collateral has been transferred out but before loanToken is pulled, violating the invariant that collateral seizure and debt repayment must be atomic, causing scoped impact: no persistent desync due to revert, but confirming that a reverting liquidate callback can DoS liquidation of an unhealthy position, leaving it unliquidated? Proof idea: deploy reverting ILiquidateCallback, call liquidate() on unhealthy borrower, assert position.debt and collateral unchanged after revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (false-return loanToken)] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that loanToken returns false on transferFrom and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom checks returndata.length == 0 || abi.decode(returndata, (bool)), trigger a revert via TransferFromReturnedFalse() in take() after position state is updated, violating the invariant that take must be atomic, causing scoped impact: take DoS for any market whose loanToken returns false on transferFrom, freezing all lending/borrowing activity? Proof idea: deploy a token that returns false on transferFrom, create market, call take(), assert revert with TransferFromReturnedFalse and all state unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / withdraw (false-return loanToken)] Can an unprivileged lender, under the precondition that loanToken returns false on transfer and SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer reverts with TransferReturnedFalse(), call withdraw() so that position.credit and marketState.withdrawable are decremented but the transfer reverts, violating the invariant that credit redemption must deliver tokens, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where lender credit is burned but tokens are not received, permanently locking lender funds? Proof idea: deploy false-return transfer token, accumulate credit via take, call withdraw(), assert revert with TransferReturnedFalse and verify credit was not decremented due to revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / withdrawCollateral (false-return collateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that collateral token returns false on transfer, call withdrawCollateral() so that position.collateral[i] is decremented and health check passes but SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse(), violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must deliver tokens, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where borrower's collateral accounting is cleared but tokens are not received? Proof idea: deploy false-return-on-transfer collateral token, supply collateral, call withdrawCollateral, assert revert and verify collateral accounting unchanged due to revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / supplyCollateral (false-return collateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that collateral token returns false on transferFrom, call supplyCollateral() so that position.collateral[i] is incremented and collateralBitmap is updated before SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse(), violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual received tokens, causing scoped impact: no persistent desync due to revert, but confirming that false-return collateral tokens cause complete DoS of supplyCollateral? Proof idea: deploy false-return-on-transferFrom collateral, call supplyCollateral, assert revert and position.collateral unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (ERC777 loanToken reentrancy)] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that loanToken is ERC777-like and calls a hook on the recipient during transfer, use the hook to reenter take() or withdraw() while the first take() has already updated position state (buyerPos.credit, sellerPos.debt, consumed) but before the outer take() completes, violating the invariant that multicall/callback/reentrancy must not break invariants that hold for single calls, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where credit or debt is double-counted or consumed tracking is bypassed? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 loanToken with tokensReceived hook that reenters take(), call outer take(), assert consumed[maker][group] and position state are consistent with a single fill.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (ERC777 collateral reentrancy via supplyCollateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that collateral token is ERC777-like with a tokensToSend hook, call supplyCollateral() which triggers the hook during SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom, allowing the hook to reenter withdrawCollateral() or take() while position.collateral[i] has been incremented but the outer supplyCollateral has not completed, violating the invariant that collateral bitmap and collateral array must be consistent, causing scoped impact: collateral accounting corruption enabling unauthorized withdrawal or health check bypass? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 collateral with tokensToSend hook that calls withdrawCollateral, call supplyCollateral, assert position.collateral[i] and collateralBitmap are consistent after all calls.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (rebasing loanToken)] Can an unprivileged attacker, under the precondition that loanToken is a rebasing token whose balances change without transfers, create a market via touchMarket() where IdLib.toId() encodes the rebasing token address, then trigger a rebase that increases Midnight's loanToken balance, violating the invariant that withdrawable must equal actual redeemable balance, causing scoped impact: lenders cannot withdraw the full rebased balance because withdrawable is not updated on rebase, effectively freezing the rebased yield? Proof idea: deploy rebasing token, create market, lend 1000e18, trigger positive rebase of 10%, call withdraw for 1100e18, assert revert because withdrawable only tracks 1000e18.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (rebasing collateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that collateral token is rebasing and position.collateral[i] is set at supply time, trigger a negative rebase that reduces Midnight's actual collateral balance below position.collateral[i], violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual deposited token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where liquidation seizes more collateral than Midnight actually holds, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to fail or drain other users' collateral? Proof idea: deploy rebasing collateral, supply 1000e18, trigger 50% negative rebase, call liquidate with seizedAssets=1000e18, assert SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer fails or balance of other users is affected.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (fee-on-transfer, settlement fee accounting)] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that loanToken is fee-on-transfer and take() computes claimableSettlementFee[offer.market.loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets before calling SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(offer.market.loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets), trigger a state where claimableSettlementFee is incremented by the full fee spread but Midnight receives only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-tokenFee), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must be backed by actual token balance, causing scoped impact: fee claimer cannot claim full settlement fees, or claiming fees drains lender withdrawable balance? Proof idea: fuzz tokenFee in [1,100] bps, execute N takes, assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] > loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) - marketState.withdrawable.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (fee-on-transfer, two-leg transfer)] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that loanToken is fee-on-transfer and take() calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) and then SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) as two separate transfers, trigger a state where the seller receives sellerAssets*(1-fee) instead of sellerAssets, violating the invariant that the seller must receive exactly sellerAssets, causing scoped impact: direct loss to the seller (maker or taker) of fee*sellerAssets per take? Proof idea: deploy 1% fee-on-transfer loanToken, execute take with sellerAssets=1000e18, assert receiver.balance(loanToken) == 990e18 not 1000e18.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (callback returns wrong magic value)] Can an unprivileged maker, under the precondition that offer.callback is set to a contract that returns a value other than CALLBACK_SUCCESS from IBuyCallback.onBuy(), cause take() to revert with WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue() after position state has been updated and consumed incremented, violating the invariant that all state changes must be atomic with callback success, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for any offer whose callback returns a non-standard value, freezing the maker's offer from being filled? Proof idea: deploy IBuyCallback returning bytes4(0xdeadbeef), call take(), assert revert with WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue and consumed unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: toId / take (sell callback returns wrong magic value)] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.callback (for a sell offer) returns a wrong value from ISellCallback.onSell(), cause take() to revert with WrongSellCallbackReturnValue() after loanToken has already been transferred from payer to Midnight and to receiver, violating the invariant that token transfers and callback success must be atomic, causing scoped impact: DoS of sell-side takes for any offer with a non-compliant sell callback, freezing seller-side liquidity? Proof idea: deploy ISellCallback returning wrong bytes4, call take() on sell offer, assert revert with WrongSellCallbackReturnValue.
